title: 2 Bedroom Houses for Rent, Cleveland West Side
metaTitle: 2 Bedroom Houses for Rent, Cleveland West Side
metaDescription: Browse 2 bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's west side, OH, with rent ranges, ZIPs, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a free showing today.
primaryKeyword: 2 bedroom houses for rent cleveland west side
secondaryKeywords: west side cleveland houses for rent
answerBox: 2-bedroom houses on Cleveland's west side can be found in ZIPs like 44102 (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway), 44109 (Old Brooklyn), and 44113 (Ohio City/Tremont). Rents for 2-bedroom homes across our Cleveland selection generally run $750–$1,100 a month, and every home we work with accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: Do you have 2-bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's west side?
body: We do, though our west-side presence is smaller than our east-side footprint. The bulk of our 90+ rental homes in Greater Cleveland sits on the east and southeast side, but we also work with a number of homes on the west side, including in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102), Old Brooklyn (44109), and Ohio City/Tremont (44113). Two-bedroom homes are our single most common size overall (51 of 91 units selection-wide), so they're the size most likely to be available on the west side at any given time.

[Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) and let our team know you're specifically looking on the west side — we can confirm what's currently available or coming soon in those ZIPs, since west-side inventory turns over less frequently than our east-side listings.

sections.h2: How much does a 2-bedroom house rent for on the west side?
body: Across our Cleveland selection as a whole (snapshot, July 2026), 2-bedroom homes have run from about $750 to $1,100 a month, averaging around $961; west-side units generally fall within that same citywide range. For broader market context, Zumper's July 2026 report lists Cleveland's overall 2-bedroom median at $1,100/mo, while RentCafe's July 2026 report shows a $1,818/mo average for 2-bedroom units in larger, professionally managed apartment communities (1,027 sq ft average) — a different, generally newer segment of the market than standalone rental houses. As with any listing, confirm the current asking rent directly with our leasing team, since figures shift as homes lease up and new ones come on the market.

headers: Typical monthly rent (our available homes, July 2026)
rows: Rare; only a couple of units selection-wide

Often includes a basement and yard

body: Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway (44102) sit along the Lake Erie shore and Detroit Avenue, with the Gordon Square Arts District nearby and Edgewater Park a short drive north. Old Brooklyn (44109) is on the city's southwest side near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo, while Ohio City and Tremont (44113) sit near-west of downtown, home to the West Side Market and served directly by the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station.

The Red Line runs from Cleveland Hopkins International Airport through these west-side neighborhoods to downtown's Tower City rail hub, and Interstates 90 and 71 provide direct driving access. For a broader view of west-side inventory beyond 2-bedroom homes, see our [houses for rent on Cleveland's west side](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-west-side-cleveland/) page, or compare with our citywide [2-bedroom house listings](/cleveland-rentals/houses/2-bedroom-houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio/), most of which are on the east side.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers on the west side
body: Every home we work with, on the west side or east side, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. One important local detail for voucher holders: Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and the City of Cleveland itself does not currently have an ordinance protecting voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination — only a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale) have enacted local protections as of July 2026. That patchwork doesn't affect how we operate, since every home we work with already welcomes vouchers, but it's worth knowing if you're apartment-hunting more broadly.

CMHA (the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority) administers the local voucher program and accepts preliminary applications year-round through its [Housing Choice Voucher page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). Our [Section 8 housing in Cleveland](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) guide covers the application and inspection process, and you can [apply online](/apply) once you've toured a home you'd like to lease.

sections.h2: What to expect when touring and applying
body: Have your paperwork ready before touring a west-side home: a government-issued photo ID, proof of income, and rental history or references. We screen every applicant the same way regardless of which side of the city the home is on — there's no no-screening path — but we work with a range of income sources, including Housing Choice Vouchers, and can talk through a co-signer if part of your income needs extra context.

West-side homes in our available homes are generally older housing stock consistent with the rest of the city's early-to-mid-1900s construction: single-family houses and duplex units, often with a basement and a small yard. Specific features like a garage, central air, or in-unit laundry vary by address, so confirm exact amenities on the current listing before scheduling a tour.

items: **Have ready for touring:** photo ID, proof of income, rental references
**Varies by listing:** garage, central air, in-unit laundry — confirm on the specific home
**Screening:** applies to every application, west side or east side; vouchers and co-signers are both workable paths

faq.q: Which west-side ZIP codes have your 2-bedroom rental homes?
faq.a: Our west-side inventory is concentrated in ZIP 44102 (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway), 44109 (Old Brooklyn), and 44113 (Ohio City/Tremont). It's a smaller share of our overall Cleveland selection than our east-side neighborhoods, so it's worth booking a showing to confirm current availability.

faq.q: How much is rent for a 2-bedroom house on the west side of Cleveland?
faq.a: Across our Cleveland selection, 2-bedroom homes have generally rented from about $750 to $1,100 a month as of a July 2026 snapshot, and west-side units typically fall within that same range depending on condition and included utilities.

faq.q: Do west-side rental homes accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with citywide, including on the west side, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Note that neither Ohio nor the City of Cleveland currently has a source-of-income law requiring other landlords to accept vouchers — some suburbs do, but that's a separate issue from our own homes.

faq.q: What's near the west-side rental homes?
faq.a: Depending on the ZIP, west-side homes are near Edgewater Park and the Gordon Square Arts District (44102), the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo (44109), or the West Side Market and RTA Red Line's Ohio City station (44113). The Red Line connects these areas to Cleveland Hopkins Airport and downtown Tower City.

faq.q: Do you have more homes on the east side than the west side?
faq.a: Yes. Our overall Cleveland-area selection is concentrated on the east and southeast side (Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, Buckeye-Shaker), with a smaller number of homes on the west side. Both sides of the city currently have 2-bedroom homes available or coming soon at times.
